sutta: the buddha said monks if any monk abuses and reviles rails at the aryans who are who are his fellows in the holy life it is utterly impossible it is unavoidable that he should not come to 1 or other of 11 disasters what 11 he fails to attain the unattained 2 from 1 he has attained he falls away 3 true dhamma is not made clear for him 4 he is conceited about true dharmas 5 he follows the holy life without delight 6 he commits some foul offense 7 he gives up the training and falls back to the low life that means the life as a layman 8 he falls into some grievous sickness 9 he goes out of his mind with distraction then he makes an end with mind confused that means he dies with mind confused 11 when body breaks up beyond death he rises up again in the ways the ill-born the downfall in hell monks if any man abuses and reviles rails at the aryans who are his fellows in the holy life it is utterly impossible it is unavoidable that he should not come to 1 or other of these 11 disasters so here that's the end of the sutra
commentary: so here the buddha is saying if we abuse a holy man and aryan here is talking about monks like if monks abuse other holy monks then they can suffer 1 of these 11 um disasters number 1 he fails to attain the unattained that means what uh holy stage yeah aryan stage he has not attained you will feel to get it now number 2 what he ascertained he falls away for example uh if there's attain concentration and then he gets angry with some some holy monks then he might lose his concentration just like devadatta devadatta at 1 time he attained all the 8 janas so that he had psychic power but later because he wanted to take over the buddha's place as leader of the monks and the buddha did not allow him so he schemed to kill the buddha and he talked bad about the buddha so all the psychic power that he had attained all fell away he lost all his psychic power number 3 true dhamma is not made clear for him he is not able to see the true dharma or he is conceited about true dharmas he is swollen hated because he knows a bit of dhamma number 5 he does not delight in the holy life that means he wears the rope he's not happy on number 6 he commits some foul offense he breaks some some precepts some heavy precepts on number 7 he gives up the training as a monk and he goes back to the lay line number 8 he becomes sick grievously sick just like devadatta after trying to kill the buddha later he became grievously ill and then he felt so felt sorry for what he did and then when he wanted to come and apologize to the buddha it was too late it's too late he died before he could see the buddha then number 9 he might go out of his mind with distraction that means his mind is very restless very scattered because of talking bad about holy man so he's very uh his mind is very scattered number 10 him he dies with mind confused when he's about to die his mind is very confused then number 11 after his dinah he might be reborn in hell because of reviling holy men [Music] now suta

items: falls away from what has been attained
true dhamma is not made clear for him
does not delight in the holy life
gives up the training and falls back to the lay life
goes out of his mind with distraction
